subject: Biology
title: The Kingdom of Fungi
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Kingdom of Fungi usable.

hook.text: Fungi are not plants. They don't photosynthesize. They digest food externally and absorb it.

buildup.text: The mushroom you see is just the fruiting body. The real organism is a network of threads underground.

discovery.text: Fungi are closer to animals than plants on the tree of life. They share a common ancestor.

twist.text: The largest organism on Earth is a honey fungus in Oregon spanning 2,385 acres.

climax.text: Fungi decompose dead matter, recycle nutrients, and keep forests alive from below the soil.

punchline.text: Mushrooms are just the tip. The real fungus hides below.

quiz.question: Are fungi more closely related to plants or animals?
